title: Cyclosorus × insularis
taxonomy: Cyclosorus × insularis is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae. This fern species is a hybrid of Cyclosorus interruptus and Cyclosorus globuliferus. It belongs to the genus Cyclosorus, which is a part of the order Polypodiales. The genus Cyclosorus itself is part of the class Polypodiopsida, and the class is further divided into the division Pteridophyta. The species is native to the islands of the Philippines.
uses: Cyclosorus × insularis has been traditionally used in various folk medicines for its potential therapeutic properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Its leaves are sometimes used to treat fever and digestive issues. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for medicinal use. Some studies suggest that compounds found in Cyclosorus × insularis may have antimicrobial and antiparasitic activities.

intro: Cyclosorus × insularis, also known as the Island Spleenwort, is a rare and endangered species of fern native to the Hawaiian Islands. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus × insularis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, Cyclosorus × insularis is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are valued for their potential therapeutic benefits.
